questions.question: Explain the FIFO principle in the context of a queue data structure.
expectedPoints: It means the first element inserted into the queue is the first element to be removed.
It is analogous to a real-world waiting line or queue of people.

questions.question: Describe the problem of memory wastage in a simple linear queue implementation using an array. How is this problem overcome?
expectedPoints: In a linear queue, 'front' and 'rear' indices move in one direction.
After several dequeue operations, 'front' moves forward, creating empty spaces at the beginning of the array.
When 'rear' reaches the last index, the queue is reported as full even though empty spaces exist at the front, leading to memory wastage.
This problem is overcome by implementing a Circular Queue.
A circular queue logically connects the last element of the array to the first element, forming a circle.
This allows the 'rear' pointer to wrap around to the start of the array to utilize the empty spaces for new enqueue operations.
